headline: Private infra, real estate capital to play larger financing role in AI data center boom, Goldman says
summary: Private infrastructure and real estate capital are expected to play a larger role in financing the ‌AI-driven data-center boom, as companies move beyond traditional forms ‌of funding, Goldman Sachs said in a note on Tuesday. Goldman increased its ​combined capex forecast for the four largest hyperscalers - Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet - to $5.3 trillion between fiscal years 2025 and 2030. Prior to the start of first-quarter earnings, the Wall Street brokerage forecast ‌capex at $4.5 trillion for ⁠the same period.

content.title: Private infra, real estate capital to play larger financing role in AI data center boom, Goldman says
content.summary: Private infrastructure and real estate capital are expected to play a larger role in financing the ‌AI-driven data-center boom, as companies move beyond traditional forms ‌of funding, Goldman Sachs said in a note on Tuesday. Prior to the start of first-quarter earnings, the Wall Street brokerage forecast ‌capex at $4.5 trillion for ⁠the same period.

headline: AT&T Stock Drops After Downgrade. Blame SpaceX.
summary: Such concerns led Oppenheimer analyst Timothy Horan to downgrade shares of  AT&T  to Perform from Outperform on Wednesday and removed his previous price target of $32.  Rising competition from providers of satellite broadband, like  Amazon  Leo and SpaceX’s Starlink, could take market share from AT&T’s internet business.  SpaceX  is expected to go public next week, and Horan says the IPO will highlight the risks satellite poses to AT&T.

SpaceX  is expected to go public next week, and Horan says the IPO will highlight the risks satellite poses to AT&T.

headline: Microsoft Says Quantum Chip Is a Big Step Forward. Not So Fast.
summary: Microsoft  says its new quantum chip, refined using artificial intelligence, brings a scalable quantum computer within reach before the end of the decade.  The company on Tuesday unveiled the latest iteration of its Majorana quantum processor, which it redesigned using AI.  Microsoft asserted that the new chip is 1,000 times more reliable than its predecessor, effectively halving the timeline to build a scalable quantum computer.

headline: S&P 500 Falls for First Time in 2 Weeks. Big Tech Is a Big Loser.
summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite fell for the first time in 10 sessions on Wednesday. The S&P 500 fell 0.7%, while the Nasdaq dropped 0.9%. The Dow fell 617 points, or 1.2%. The big three indexes also snapped a streak of five sessions in which they all finished the day with closing highs.

content.title: S&P 500 Falls for First Time in 2 Weeks. Big Tech Is a Big Loser.
content.summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite fell for the first time in 10 sessions on Wednesday. The S&P 500 fell 0.7%, while the Nasdaq dropped 0.9%. The Dow fell 617 points, or 1.2%. The big three indexes also snapped a streak of five sessions in which they all finished the day with closing highs.

headline: Exclusive-Uber's commitment to self-driving startup Nuro is close to $500 million, sources say
summary: Uber has committed close to half a billion dollars in self-driving startup Nuro, two sources directly aware of the matter told Reuters, detailing the ‌extent of a major investment by the ride-hailing company in developing commercial robotaxis.  Uber has ​been positioning itself as a platform for the nascent industry and has partnered with many autonomous vehicle companies, including Chinese tech giant Baidu, U.S. electric vehicle maker Rivian, and British startup Wayve.  One of Uber's commitments is a three-way partnership with Nuro and electric vehicle company Lucid to roll ‌out 35,000 robotaxis using Lucid's Gravity ⁠SUVs and upcoming midsize vehicles, Nuro's technology and Uber's platform.

content.title: Exclusive-Uber's commitment to self-driving startup Nuro is close to $500 million, sources say
content.summary: Uber has committed close to half a billion dollars in self-driving startup Nuro, two sources directly aware of the matter told Reuters, detailing the ‌extent of a major investment by the ride-hailing company in developing commercial robotaxis.  Uber has ​been positioning itself as a platform for the nascent industry and has partnered with many autonomous vehicle companies, including Chinese tech giant Baidu, U.S. electric vehicle maker Rivian, and British startup Wayve.  One of Uber's commitments is a three-way partnership with Nuro and electric vehicle company Lucid to roll ‌out 35,000 robotaxis using Lucid's Gravity ⁠SUVs and upcoming midsize vehicles, Nuro's technology and Uber's platform.
